patch.value: context-pack accepts --neighborhood-depth <n> (default 1, cap 5); depth 1 is byte-identical to prior output; depth 0 == --without-neighborhood; depth 2+ includes transitive neighbors via BFS over the dependency graph (both directions); neighbors de-duplicated; a focus item is never its own neighbor; --include-closed neighbor behavior unchanged; tests + README updated; release:check green

value.author: codex
value.text:
Design + implementation notes:

NEIGHBORHOOD AS BFS: buildContextPack now resolves a numeric depth (default 1). Starting frontier = focus item ids. Each hop scans all relationships (extracted via extractRelationships from deps/dependencies/blocked_by/blockedBy) and expands BOTH directions: if frontier contains rel.from add rel.to, and if frontier contains rel.to add rel.from. A visited set seeded with the focus ids prevents revisiting and guarantees a focus item is never emitted as its own neighbor. Newly-reached ids each hop become the next frontier; all are accumulated into neighborIds. After traversal, focus ids are removed from neighborIds (belt-and-suspenders). Neighbors found at any hop depth are classified as neighbors, never focus.

DEPTH SEMANTICS: --neighborhood-depth <n> default 1. depth 0 produces zero neighbors and is equivalent to --without-neighborhood (which forces depth 0; --without-neighborhood wins if both given). Values are clamped: <0 -> 0, >5 -> 5 (MAX_NEIGHBORHOOD_DEPTH=5) to bound traversal. Parsed via intOptionMin0 (rejects negatives/non-numeric with USAGE exit), accepts both kebab and camelCase option keys.

BYTE-IDENTICAL AT DEPTH 1: The only output field touched is filters.neighborhood, now derived as (depth > 0). At default/depth-1 that is true (matching the old options.neighborhood !== false); at depth 0 / --without-neighborhood it is false (matching old behavior). No new fields added to the ContextPack output shape, so JSON and markdown packs are byte-identical at depth 1. Proven by two tests asserting JSON.stringify and renderMarkdown equality between {default} and {neighborhoodDepth:1} for a 2-hop chain fixture.

--INCLUDE-CLOSED: unchanged. Closed filtering only ever applied to focus selection via matchesFilters; neighbors were and still are pulled purely from the relationship graph regardless of closed status. BFS preserves that exactly.

TESTS (7 new, all green, 17 total pass / 0 fail):
- depth 1 (explicit) JSON byte-identical to legacy default
- renderMarkdown byte-identical at depth 1 vs default
- depth 1 includes only direct neighbors (pm-2, not pm-4)
- depth 2 pulls neighbor-of-a-neighbor (pm-4) that depth 1 omits
- depth 0 == --without-neighborhood (zero neighbors, filters.neighborhood false, identical JSON)
- cap enforced (depth 999 bounded; MAX_NEIGHBORHOOD_DEPTH==5)
- focus item never its own neighbor (focus pm-1+pm-2 sharing an edge yields only pm-4)

Fixture: pm-1 -depends_on-> pm-2 -depends_on-> pm-4, plus unrelated pm-5. README documents the flag, default, cap, BFS semantics, and the depth-1-unchanged guarantee.

patch.value: Implemented neighborhood-depth BFS context expansion with tests and docs
